title: Scrub ~ from /root value in push
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

If the user supplies a tilde ('~') in the destination for push, replace the tilde with the value of the HOME environment variable.   This addresses #777.  As @pixdrift points out there, gnu spec states that the tilde won't be converted unless it's at the start of the string.  I've added this as a possible convenience for the end user.  No harm if we decide to stick with the gnu thinking and burn this pr.

Testing:
```
Before fix:
# buildah push alpine oci:~/tom.oci:latest
lstat /root/~: no such file or directory

After Fix:
# buildah push alpine oci:~/tom.oci:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:cd7100a72410606589a54b932cabd804a17f9ae5b42a1882bd56d263e02b6215
 4.20 MiB / 4.20 MiB [======================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:b1a7f144ece0194921befe57ab30ed1fd98c5950db7996719429020986092058
 585 B / 585 B [============================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

comments.body: One nit, but LGTM with or without that change.  I've no idea how prevalent the usage pattern is.

Overall, please don’t do it here.

The _transport_`:`_reference_ syntax is intended to be the same everywhere, and locally editing the strings like that would make it inconsistent.  (Inconsistent already within `buildah`, e.g. this does not change `buildah from`; and inconsistent with other users like `podman`/`skopeo`.)

If this should happen at all, it should happen in containers/image, in the relevant transports’ parsers (maybe shared in `c/image/directory/explicitfilepath`, maybe in a new helper). 

As for whether it should happen at all… I don’t know; I appreciate the convenience, OTOH it is one more metacharacter / special string parsing situation that everyone would need to take into account (scripts would now have to `s,\([^/].*),./\1,` to correctly protect user input), and most won’t do that; meanwhile, typing `$HOME` is not _that_ much work. I’m leaning towards no—but I know I am terrible about UIs.

Thanks all for the looksee and thoughts.  No fouls on killing this PR, I kept it simple just to see the discussion as I was a fence sitter.  I think what I'll do is add a "known issue" in our Known Issue page and  remind folks about $HOME.
